title: Tanker report - Week 31
summary:
VLCC
Rates from the Middle East Gulf have remained flat this week, with 280,000mt Middle East Gulf to USG via the Cape/Cape routing still being assessed at WS21-22 level and 270,000mt to China languishing at WS36. In the Atlantic, 260,000mt West Africa to China tiptoed up a point to WS39.5-40 region while 270,000mt US Gulf to China was $300k weaker at $5.33m. Suezmax
Rates for 135,000mt Black Sea/Med remained static at WS55, while 130,000mt West Africa/UKContinent managed to gain about four points early in the week to the low WS50s.

children.title: VLCC
children.summary: Rates from the Middle East Gulf have remained flat this week, with 280,000mt Middle East Gulf to USG via the Cape/Cape routing still being assessed at WS21-22 level and 270,000mt to China languishing at WS36. In the Atlantic, 260,000mt West Africa to China tiptoed up a point to WS39.5-40 region while 270,000mt US Gulf to China was $300k weaker at $5.33m.

children.title: Suezmax
children.summary: Rates for 135,000mt Black Sea/Med remained static at WS55, while 130,000mt West Africa/UKContinent managed to gain about four points early in the week to the low WS50s. However, the market has fallen back now to about WS48, which is still up a point from a week ago.

children.title: Aframax
children.summary: In the Mediterranean, market rates have stumbled again losing six points to WS60 for 80,000mt Ceyhan/Med. In Northern Europe, rates for 80,000mt Hound Point/UKCont have dipped 2.5 points to WS77.5, while 100,000mt Baltic/UKCont fell four points to WS52.5.

children.title: Clean
children.summary: In the Middle East Gulf, rates for 75,000mt to Japan have been under pressure. After starting the week in the very low WS60S, a run to Japan was fixed at WS57.5 with charterers looking to test rates further.
